pr.title: some grgreatbay data matching + attribute labelling
pr.author: MarioLover63

summary: Great Bay stage cleanup focused on data matching and semantic attribute labels in src/melee/gr/grgreatbay.c. The PR renamed grGb_StageAttr fields from raw x-offset names into moon, floating-floor, kame/turtle, direction-probability, item-probability, and item-weight fields; added StageCallbacks role comments; defined previously extern Vec3/SpawnItem data; and made small stack/padding adjustments. decomp-dev reported the grgreatbay unit's .data, .rodata, and .sdata reaching 100% matched, plus small improvements in grGreatBay_801F499C, grGreatBay_801F63F4, and grGreatBay_801F60C4.
what_changed: Added inline labels to grGb_803E3E84 StageCallbacks entries for Great Bay objects: turtle, main stage, background, foreground water, giant 1-4, moon, and tingle.

Renamed grGb_StageAttr fields from raw x0/x2/x4-style names to semantic names such as moon_fall_wait_a/b, floatfloor_landing_rate, floatfloor_slant_mul/add/limit/rate/reb_rate, floatfloor_slide_* fields, floatfloor_down_* fields, kame_wait_frame_a/b, kame_rebirth_frame_a/b, kame_x/y, kame_x_offset_init, kame_x_lr_offset_a/b, kame_x_fb_offset_a/b, kame_scale, kame_ud_scale, kame_dir_prob[4], kame_item_prob, and items[10].
Updated all grGb_804D69E0.x0 attribute accesses in turtle/kame setup and update code, moon timer code, floating-floor physics, direction randomization, and item spawning to use the new names.
Renamed a local HSD_JObj pointer in grGreatBay_801F499C from jobj2 to trans_jobj, clarifying that it is the transform JObj used for translation/rotation work.
Replaced extern declarations for grGb_803B81C8 and grGb_803B81D4 with concrete const definitions: Vec3 grGb_803B81C8 = {0.99f, 35.72f, 0.0f} and a SpawnItem initializer.
Adjusted matching scaffolding by changing PAD_STACK(12) to PAD_STACK(4) in grGreatBay_801F60C4 and adding an s32 padding local while changing PAD_STACK(8) to PAD_STACK(4) in grGreatBay_801F63F4.
Kept existing offset / 4 item-loop indexing while renaming x7C to items, preserving the suspicious-but-matching loop shape rather than simplifying it.
smart_moves: The attribute struct kept explicit offset comments while replacing opaque xNN field names, making the layout auditable and safer for future data work.
Field names were grouped by behavior instead of only by object: moon timers, floating-floor response, turtle/kame behavior, direction probabilities, item probability, and weighted item table.
The PR defined actual small data objects instead of leaving them as externs, which directly contributed to data-section matching according to the bot report.
StageCallbacks comments provided a low-risk map from callback rows to in-game objects without changing codegen.
Stack-layout fixes were minimal and localized, using PAD_STACK adjustments and one padding local rather than rewriting control flow.
decomp_lessons: When a stage attribute blob is understood, rename fields at the struct definition and propagate those names through all consumers; this turns repeated xNN accesses into documentation for the behavior.
Keep offset comments on partially understood binary-layout structs after renaming fields so future decompilers can verify that semantic names did not disturb layout.
For stage data matching, small const objects such as Vec3 and SpawnItem initializers may need to live in the same translation unit instead of remaining unresolved externs.
Do not simplify odd loops or indexing patterns during a matching PR unless codegen is verified; this PR preserved offset / 4 access patterns in the item-weight loops.
PAD_STACK size and dummy local variables can be legitimate final-mile matching tools for stubborn functions, especially after data and struct cleanup has changed local layout pressure.
naming_conventions: Use subsystem prefixes for stage attribute fields: moon_, floatfloor_, and kame_.
Use suffixes like _mul, _add, _limit, _rate, and _reb_rate for physics tuning constants where the consuming expression shows the role.
Use _a and _b for paired random-range or timing fields when the exact high/low semantic name is not asserted in the PR.
Use kame_dir_prob for direction-probability arrays and kame_item_prob plus items[] for weighted item-spawn data.
Inline comments on StageCallbacks rows can document stage object roles when function names remain address-based.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted grGb_803B81C8 and grGb_803B81D4 from extern declarations to const definitions, after which decomp-dev reported grgreatbay .rodata, .sdata, and .data reaching 100%.
Adjusted PAD_STACK in grGreatBay_801F60C4 and grGreatBay_801F63F4, including an explicit s32 padding local in grGreatBay_801F63F4, to improve function matching without altering behavior.
Preserved existing control-flow and indexing shapes, including the offset accumulator and offset / 4 array access in grGreatBay_801F63F4.
Used semantic renames only where existing code usage supported the meaning, leaving function names address-based.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched data improved to 38.10% overall (+0.05%, +632 bytes).
Bot-reported new matches: main/melee/gr/grgreatbay .data +155 bytes, 70.26% to 100.00%; .rodata +88 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%; .sdata +5 bytes, 81.82% to 100.00%.
Bot-reported function improvements: grGreatBay_801F499C +2 bytes, 99.90% to 99.99%; grGreatBay_801F63F4 +1 byte, 96.63% to 96.72%; grGreatBay_801F60C4 94.51% to 94.67%.
